meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Student Guide: Analytics Setup for Your First SaaS App
content.seo_description: Stop guessing if users like your app. Learn to implement professional analytics to track growth and validate your SaaS project using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier, implement event-driven analytics immediately. By integrating tracking into your core user flows, you transform raw traffic into actionable data. Use Antigravity for rapid instrumentation and Claude Code to automate the deployment of your event-tracking schema across your entire production codebase.
content.intro:
You’ve pushed your first lines of code to production, but the silence is deafening. You see the dashboard, but you don't know who is using your features or where they drop off. That empty feeling of building in the dark is the single greatest hurdle for student developers transitioning into SaaS engineering.

Today, we stop guessing. We are moving from 'hoping for users' to 'engineering for growth.' By integrating a robust analytics layer into your architecture, you won't just build a project—you'll build a data-backed product that proves your value to future employers or investors.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that defines success for your user. For a SaaS app, this is usually 'Account Created' or 'First API Call'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest 5 key events I should track to measure user retention and feature adoption for a student-built SaaS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify key user events and generate a JSON schema for tracking'

steps.title: Instrument the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Initialize your analytics SDK (like PostHog or Mixpanel) in your app's entry point to capture session data without bloating your main logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable wrapper for a tracking library that handles user identity and event properties automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'initialize analytics provider in root app component'

steps.title: Automate Event Triggers
steps.description: Hook your analytics into your UI interactions. Don't manual-code every click; use a centralized utility to keep your code clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom React hook named useAnalytics that wraps tracking calls and injects standard metadata like timestamps and user roles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create useAnalytics hook and implement in login flow'

steps.title: Implement Server-Side Tracking
steps.description: Ensure critical actions (like payments or database updates) are tracked server-side to prevent data loss from ad-blockers or browser refreshes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my existing backend API routes to dispatch an event to the analytics API after a successful database transaction.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'add server-side analytics event firing to all protected API routes'

steps.title: Validate Data Pipeline
steps.description: Verify that your events are firing correctly in your dashboard and that the payload structure matches your initial schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite that simulates user actions and verifies that the correct events are dispatched to the tracking endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute analytics integration tests and report failures'

pro_tips: Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your analytics events; it’s a security liability and violates GDPR.
Use distinct naming conventions like 'User_Signed_Up' vs 'user-signed-up' and stick to them strictly across your entire project.
Implement 'Autocapture' sparingly; it’s great for discovery but leads to noisy, unmanageable data if you don't filter it.
Track the 'empty state' views—it tells you more about user frustration than successful actions do.
Create a 'Dashboard Watchlist' where you check your key metrics every morning for 5 minutes; it builds the habit of data-driven development.
content.next_steps: Now that you have data flowing, set up a simple weekly email alert that sends your top 3 metrics to your inbox. This shifts your focus from 'coding features' to 'driving outcomes'.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build a professional-grade SaaS that tracks its own success. Stop building blindly and start shipping with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Initialize your analytics SDK (like PostHog or Mixpanel) in your app's entry point to capture session data without bloating your main logic.
